Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Compact Child Issues into Parent Issues

## Context

The `.issues/` directory has 2 parent issues and 21 child issues. The user wants all child issue content compacted into their respective parent issues, then the child issue files deleted.

## Parent-Child Mapping

### Parent 1: `feature-git-abstraction-layer.md` (12 children)
**Layer 1:**
- `feature-git-abstraction-layer--module-scaffolding.md`
- `feature-git-abstraction-layer--data-types.md`
- `feature-git-abstraction-layer--error-types.md`
- `feature-git-abstraction-layer--repository-trait.md`
- `feature-git-abstraction-layer--real-repo-readonly.md`
- `feature-git-abstraction-layer--real-repo-mutating.md`
- `feature-git-abstraction-layer--fake-repository.md`

**Layer 2:**
- `feature-git-abstraction-layer--repository-entity.md`
- `feature-git-abstraction-layer--git-store-entity.md`
- `feature-git-abstraction-layer--events-optimistic-ui.md`

**Layer 3:**
- `feature-git-abstraction-layer--buffer-diff-entity.md`
- `feature-git-abstraction-layer--secondary-hunk-status.md`

### Parent 2: `feature-macos-app-foundation.md` (9 children)
**Foundation:**
- `debt-macos-app-foundation--pin-gpui-dependency.md`
- `feature-macos-app-foundation--asset-pipeline-and-fonts.md`
- `feature-macos-app-foundation--app-entry-point-and-window.md`

**Integration:**
- `feature-macos-app-foundation--theme-system.md`
- `feature-macos-app-foundation--actions-and-keybindings.md`

**Git Panel:**
- `feature-macos-app-foundation--git-panel-scaffolding.md`
- `feature-macos-app-foundation--staging-interactions.md`
- `feature-macos-app-foundation--commit-message-workflow.md`
- `feature-macos-app-foundation--panel-toolbar.md`

## Steps

1. **Rewrite `feature-git-abstraction-layer.md`** — Keep existing parent metadata, summary, user story, dependency graph, design considerations, and out of scope. Replace the "Child Issues" section with inline child issue content (each child becomes a subsection under its layer heading, preserving summary, detailed description, success criteria, test guidance, and design considerations from each child).

2. **Rewrite `feature-macos-app-foundation.md`** — Same approach. Keep parent structure, inline all 9 children as subsections.

3. **Delete all 21 child issue files** using `rm`.

## Format for Inlined Children

Each child becomes a section like:

```markdown
### Child Title

| Field | Value |
| ... | ... |

**Summary:** ...

**Detailed Description:** ...

**Success Criteria:** ...

**Design Considerations:** ...

**Test Guidance:** ...
```

Keep all substantive content. Drop "Definition of Done" (identical boilerplate across all children) and "Out of Scope" (context is clear from parent). Keep "User Story" only if it adds unique context.

## Verification

- Confirm both parent files contain all child content
- Confirm all 21 child files are deleted
- Confirm only 2 files remain in `.issues/`


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/denny/.claude/projects/-Users-denny-Documents-repos-cupel/6c9f4580-e2eb-4c1a-ba2a-300e3d244d66.jsonl

## Context

The `.issues/` directory has 2 parent issues and 21 child issues. The user wants all child issue content compacted into their respective parent issues, then the child issue files deleted.

## Parent-Child Mapping

### Parent 2: `feature-macos-app-foundation.md` (9 children)

## Steps

1. **Rewrite `feature-git-abstraction-layer.md`** — Keep existing parent metadata, summary, user story, dependency graph, design considerations, and out of scope. Replace the "Child Issues" section with inline child issue content (each child becomes a subsection under its layer heading, preserving summary, detailed description, success criteria, test guidance, and design considerations from each child).

2. **Rewrite `feature-macos-app-foundation.md`** — Same approach. Keep parent structure, inline all 9 children as subsections.

3. **Delete all 21 child issue files** using `rm`.

## Format for Inlined Children

Each child becomes a section like:

```markdown
### Child Title

| Field | Value |
| ... | ... |

**Summary:** ...

**Detailed Description:** ...

**Success Criteria:** ...

**Design Considerations:** ...

**Test Guidance:** ...
```

Keep all substantive content. Drop "Definition of Done" (identical boilerplate across all children) and "Out of Scope" (context is clear from parent). Keep "User Story" only if it adds unique context.

## Verification

- Confirm both parent files contain all child content
- Confirm all 21 child files are deleted
- Confirm only 2 files remain in `.issues/`

# Git Abstraction Layer

| Field        | Value       |
| ------------ | ----------- |
| **Type**     | `feature`   |
| **Priority** | `p1`        |
| **Parent**   | —           |
| **Created**  | 2026-03-13  |

## Summary

Build a three-layer git abstraction for cupel modeled after Zed's git crates: a pure git2/CLI wrapper (`src/git/`), GPUI entity wrappers (`GitStore`, `Repository`), and per-buffer diff state (`BufferDiff`). This is the foundational infrastructure that all git UI features depend on.

## Child Issues

### Layer 1 — Pure Git Abstraction

- [ ] [Module scaffolding](feature-git-abstraction-layer--module-scaffolding.md)
- [ ] [Data types](feature-git-abstraction-layer--data-types.md)
- [ ] [Error types](feature-git-abstraction-layer--error-types.md)
- [ ] [Repository trait](feature-git-abstraction-layer--repository-trait.md)
- [ ] [Real repo read-only methods](feature-git-abstraction-layer--real-repo-readonly.md)
- [ ] [Real repo mutating methods](feature-git-abstraction-layer--real-repo-mutating.md)
- [ ] [Fake repository test double](feature-git-abstraction-layer--fake-repository.md)

### Layer 2 — GitStore & Repository Entities

- [ ] [Repository entity](feature-git-abstraction-layer--repository-entity.md)
- [ ] [GitStore entity](feature-git-abstraction-layer--git-store-entity.md)
- [ ] [Events & optimistic UI](feature-git-abstraction-layer--events-optimistic-ui.md)

### Layer 3 — Buffer Diff

- [ ] [BufferDiff entity](feature-git-abstraction-layer--buffer-diff-entity.md)
- [ ] [Secondary hunk status](feature-git-abstraction-layer--secondary-hunk-status.md)

## User Story

As a cupel developer, I want a layered git abstraction so that git operations are testable, async-safe, and decoupled from the UI layer.

## Dependency Graph

```
#1 (scaffolding)
├── #2 (data types)  ─┐
├── #3 (error types) ─┤
│                      ├── #4 (trait) ──┬── #5 (real readonly) ── #6 (real mutating) ──┐
│                      │                └── #7 (fake repo) ────────────────────────────┤
│                      │                                                                ├── #8 (repo entity) ── #9 (git store) ── #10 (events/optimistic)
└── #11 (buffer diff, only needs imara-diff dep) ── #12 (secondary hunk status, also needs #10)
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `src/git/` module compiles with all types, traits, and implementations.
- [ ] `GitRepository` trait has ~40 methods covering status, branch, blame, staging, commit, push/pull, stash, log, and conflict resolution.
- [ ] `RealGitRepository` wraps git2 for local ops and shells out to `git` CLI for network ops.
- [ ] `FakeGitRepository` enables fully in-memory testing without a real repo.
- [ ] `Repository` GPUI entity serializes git ops through an async job queue.
- [ ] `GitStore` GPUI entity acts as a repository registry with event emission.
- [ ] `BufferDiff` entity keeps diff hunks up-to-date as buffers change.
- [ ] `SecondaryHunkStatus` enables staging-aware diff display.
- [ ] All layers compile and pass tests independently.

## Design Considerations

- Follow Zed's layered architecture: `git` (pure) → `project` (entities) → `git_ui` (rendering).
- The pure layer has no GPUI, no async runtime — just synchronous trait methods.
- `git2::Repository` is not `Sync`; wrap in `Mutex` inside `RealGitRepository`.
- Network operations (push/pull/fetch) use `git` CLI subprocess, not git2.
- Use `imara-diff` for the diff algorithm (replaces `similar`).
- The `env: &HashMap<String, String>` parameter pattern on mutating methods supports future credential helper injection.

## Out of Scope

- Git UI panel (GitPanel, ProjectDiff, ConflictView) — that's a separate epic.
- AskPass credential helper binary — can use empty env for local-only operations initially.
- Remote mode (`GitStoreMode::Remote`) and protobuf RPC — local-only for Phase 0.
- Worktree scanner integration — repositories will be added manually for now.

## Reference

- Architecture reference: [.epic/git.md](../.epic/git.md)

file.content:
# macOS App Foundation

| Field        | Value       |
| ------------ | ----------- |
| **Type**     | `feature`   |
| **Priority** | `p1`        |
| **Parent**   | —           |
| **Created**  | 2026-03-13  |

## Summary

Get a styled, interactive GPUI window on screen with a functional git panel. This covers the initialization prerequisites identified in the [macOS app epic](../.epic/macos_app.md) and the first real UI component — a git panel that displays changed files, supports staging/unstaging, and allows committing.

## Current State

The git abstraction layer (`src/git/`) is substantially implemented and already uses GPUI entities (`Entity<Repository>`, `Entity<GitStore>`) with an async job queue via `cx.spawn()` and `tokio::sync::mpsc`. The app foundation (dependency pinning, assets, entry point, theme, keybindings) is implemented and compiles. The window opens with a placeholder view.

## Child Issues

### Foundation — Sequential

- [x] [Pin GPUI dependency](debt-macos-app-foundation--pin-gpui-dependency.md)
- [x] [Asset pipeline and fonts](feature-macos-app-foundation--asset-pipeline-and-fonts.md)
- [x] [App entry point and window](feature-macos-app-foundation--app-entry-point-and-window.md)

### Integration — Parallel (after entry point)

- [x] [Theme system](feature-macos-app-foundation--theme-system.md)
- [x] [Actions and keybindings](feature-macos-app-foundation--actions-and-keybindings.md)

### Git Panel — Sequential (after foundation)

- [ ] [GitPanel scaffolding and file list](feature-macos-app-foundation--git-panel-scaffolding.md)
- [ ] [Staging interactions](feature-macos-app-foundation--staging-interactions.md)
- [ ] [Commit message and workflow](feature-macos-app-foundation--commit-message-workflow.md)
- [ ] [Panel toolbar with branch info](feature-macos-app-foundation--panel-toolbar.md)

## User Story

As a cupel developer, I want a working GPUI window with a git panel that shows changed files, supports staging, and allows committing so that the app has its first meaningful interactive feature.

## Dependency Graph

```
#1 (pin gpui) → #2 (assets/fonts) → #3 (entry point/window)
                                          ├── #4 (theme system)
                                          └── #5 (actions/keybindings)
                                                      │
                                          #6 (git panel scaffolding)
                                            │
                                          #7 (staging interactions)
                                            │
                                          #8 (commit message/workflow)
                                            │
                                          #9 (panel toolbar)
```

## Success Criteria

- [x] GPUI is pinned in `Cargo.toml` with `rust-embed` and `anyhow` added.
- [x] At least one font is embedded via `rust-embed` and served by an `AssetSource` implementation.
- [x] `main.rs` boots the app, opens a window, and renders a root view.
- [x] A `Theme` struct is registered as a `Global` with a dark palette.
- [x] At least `Quit` (cmd-q) is bound via the `actions!` macro.
- [ ] `AppView` owns an `Entity<GitStore>` and renders a `GitPanel` child.
- [ ] Changed files are listed, grouped by staging state (staged / unstaged).
- [ ] Files can be staged/unstaged via click and keyboard shortcuts.
- [ ] A commit message input and commit button are present and functional.
- [ ] The toolbar displays the current branch name and changed file counts.
- [ ] The app compiles, launches, and renders a styled, interactive git panel.

## Design Considerations

- Follow the patterns described in [.epic/macos_app.md](../.epic/macos_app.md) and [.epic/git.md](../.epic/git.md).
- GPUI is not a stable public API — pin to a specific version and treat updates as deliberate maintenance.
- The existing git entities already use `cx.spawn()` with `tokio` channels for background work — no additional executor setup needed.
- `GitPanel` subscribes to `GitStoreEvent` and re-renders when statuses change.
- The panel reads `Repository` state via `repo.read(cx)` — never calls `git2` directly.

## Out of Scope

- ProjectDiff view (full diff viewer) — separate epic.
- ConflictView — separate epic.
- Multiple windows or split views.
- Light theme or theme switching.
- Hunk-level staging (uses `set_index_text`; complex UI).
- Push/pull execution from toolbar (display-only for now).

## Reference

- Architecture reference: [.epic/macos_app.md](../.epic/macos_app.md)

# Module Scaffolding

| Field        | Value                                                              |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Type**     | `feature`                                                          |
| **Priority** | `p1`                                                               |
| **Parent**   | [Git Abstraction Layer](feature-git-abstraction-layer.md)          |
| **Created**  | 2026-03-13                                                         |

## Summary

Create the `src/git/` module layout with `mod.rs` and empty submodule files, and add the `git2` and `imara-diff` dependencies to `Cargo.toml`. This is the foundation that all other git issues build on.

## User Story

As a developer, I want the module structure and dependencies in place so that subsequent issues can implement types and traits without setup overhead.

## Detailed Description

Replace the current `src/git.rs` stub with a `src/git/` directory containing:

```
src/git/
├── mod.rs           # re-exports
├── repository.rs    # GitRepository trait (issue #4)
├── real_repo.rs     # RealGitRepository (issues #5, #6)
├── fake_repo.rs     # FakeGitRepository (issue #7)
├── types.rs         # data types (issue #2)
├── status.rs        # StatusEntry + related types
├── error.rs         # GitError (issue #3)
└── diff.rs          # BufferDiff types (issue #11)
```

Add to `Cargo.toml` dependencies:
- `git2` — libgit2 bindings
- `imara-diff` — diff algorithm

Wire `mod git` into `src/lib.rs`.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `src/git.rs` is replaced by `src/git/mod.rs` with submodule declarations.
- [ ] All submodule files exist (can be empty or contain minimal placeholder structs).
- [ ] `git2` and `imara-diff` are added to `[dependencies]` in `Cargo.toml`.
- [ ] `cargo check` passes.
- [ ] `src/lib.rs` declares `pub mod git`.

## Design Considerations

- Keep `mod.rs` re-exports minimal for now; each issue will add its own public items.
- Use the same module naming as Zed's `crates/git/src/` for familiarity.
- The `diff.rs` module is for buffer-diff types used in Layer 3; it only needs the `imara-diff` dep from this issue.

## Out of Scope

- Implementing any types or traits — that's issues #2–#7 and #11–#12.
- Adding GPUI-related dependencies — those belong in Layer 2 issues.

## Test Guidance

**Primary category:** Integration tests

**What to test:**

- `cargo check` passes with the new module structure.
- All modules are importable from `cupel::git::*`.

**Boundary conditions:**

- Ensure no circular dependencies between submodules.

**Infrastructure needs:**

- None beyond standard `cargo`.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Implementation is complete and builds without errors.
- [ ] All new and existing tests pass in CI.
- [ ] Code has been reviewed and approved by at least one other engineer.
- [ ] Changes are documented — at minimum, meaningful commit messages and inline comments where intent is non-obvious.
- [ ] All success criteria listed in the issue are met.

These are the value types used throughout the git abstraction.
    13→
    14→## User Story
    15→
    16→As a developer implementing the `GitRepository` trait, I want well-defined data types so that method signatures are concrete and the domain model is clear.
    17→
    18→## Detailed Description
    19→
    20→### `src/git/types.rs`
    21→
    22→```rust
    23→pub struct RepoPath(Arc<Path>);                // repo-relative path, implements Ord
    24→pub struct Branch { name, upstream, is_head, unix_timestamp }
    25→pub struct UpstreamBranch { name, ahead, behind }
    26→pub struct BlameEntry { sha, line_range, author, author_mail, author_time, committer, summary }
    27→pub struct CommitOptions { amend, signoff }
    28→pub struct PushOptions { force, set_upstream }
    29→pub struct CommitSummary { sha, message, author, timestamp }
    30→pub struct CommitDetails { summary, diff_stats, parent_shas }
    31→pub struct StashEntry { index, message, sha }
    32→pub enum ConflictSide { Ours, Theirs, Base }
    33→```
    34→
    35→### `src/git/status.rs`
    36→
    37→```rust
    38→pub struct StatusEntry { repo_path, status }
    39→pub struct GitFileStatus { index_status, worktree_status, conflict }
    40→pub enum FileStatus { Added, Modified, Deleted, Untracked, Unchanged }
    41→```
    42→
    43→`RepoPath` should implement `Clone`, `Debug`, `PartialEq`, `Eq`, `PartialOrd`, `Ord`, `Hash`, and `Display`. It wraps `Arc<Path>` for cheap cloning.
    44→
    45→`StatusEntry` staging state is derived from comparing `index_status` and `worktree_status` — provide a helper method `staging_state()` or similar.
    46→
    47→## Success Criteria
    48→
    49→- [ ] All listed types are defined with appropriate derives.
    50→- [ ] `RepoPath` implements `Ord`, `Hash`, `Display`, and `From<&str>` / `From<PathBuf>`.
    51→- [ ] `StatusEntry` has a method to derive staging state (fully staged, fully unstaged, partially staged).
    52→- [ ] `FileStatus` enum covers Added, Modified, Deleted, Untracked, Unchanged.
    53→- [ ] `cargo check` passes.
    54→
    55→## Design Considerations
    56→
    57→- Use `Arc<Path>` inside `RepoPath` for cheap cloning — these are passed around frequently.
    58→- Keep types `Send + Sync` since they cross thread boundaries via the job queue.
    59→- Don't add SumTree integration in this issue — that's an optimization for later.
    60→- `BlameEntry` uses `String` for SHA rather than a git2-specific OID type to keep the pure layer backend-agnostic.
    61→
    62→## Out of Scope
    63→
    64→- SumTree `Item` / `Summary` implementations for `StatusEntry` — future optimization.
    65→- Conversion between `RepoPath` and absolute/project paths — handled by GitStore (issue #9).
    66→

    70→
    71→**What to test:**
    72→
    73→- `RepoPath` ordering matches lexicographic path ordering.
    74→- `RepoPath` round-trips through `Display` / `From<&str>`.
    75→- `StatusEntry::staging_state()` returns correct state for all combinations of `index_status` and `worktree_status`.
    76→
    77→**Boundary conditions:**
    78→
    79→- `RepoPath` with empty path, paths with special characters, nested paths.
    80→- `GitFileStatus` where both `index_status` and `worktree_status` are `Unchanged`.
    81→
    82→**Infrastructure needs:**
    83→
    84→- None.
    85→
    86→## Definition of Done
    87→
    88→- [ ] Implementation is complete and builds without errors.
    89→- [ ] All new and existing tests pass in CI.
    90→- [ ] Code has been reviewed and approved by at least one other engineer.
    91→- [ ] Changes are documented — at minimum, meaningful commit messages and inline comments where intent is non-obvious.
    92→- [ ] All success criteria listed in the issue are met.

# Data Types

| Field        | Value                                                              |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Type**     | `feature`                                                          |
| **Priority** | `p1`                                                               |
| **Parent**   | [Git Abstraction Layer](feature-git-abstraction-layer.md)          |
| **Created**  | 2026-03-13                                                         |

## Summary

Define the core git data types in `src/git/types.rs` and `src/git/status.rs`: `RepoPath`, `StatusEntry`, `GitFileStatus`, `FileStatus`, `Branch`, `UpstreamBranch`, `BlameEntry`, `CommitOptions`, `CommitSummary`, `CommitDetails`, `StashEntry`, `ConflictSide`, and `PushOptions`. These are the value types used throughout the git abstraction.

## User Story

As a developer implementing the `GitRepository` trait, I want well-defined data types so that method signatures are concrete and the domain model is clear.

## Detailed Description

### `src/git/types.rs`

```rust
pub struct RepoPath(Arc<Path>);                // repo-relative path, implements Ord
pub struct Branch { name, upstream, is_head, unix_timestamp }
pub struct UpstreamBranch { name, ahead, behind }
pub struct BlameEntry { sha, line_range, author, author_mail, author_time, committer, summary }
pub struct CommitOptions { amend, signoff }
pub struct PushOptions { force, set_upstream }
pub struct CommitSummary { sha, message, author, timestamp }
pub struct CommitDetails { summary, diff_stats, parent_shas }
pub struct StashEntry { index, message, sha }
pub enum ConflictSide { Ours, Theirs, Base }
```

### `src/git/status.rs`

```rust
pub struct StatusEntry { repo_path, status }
pub struct GitFileStatus { index_status, worktree_status, conflict }
pub enum FileStatus { Added, Modified, Deleted, Untracked, Unchanged }
```

`RepoPath` should implement `Clone`, `Debug`, `PartialEq`, `Eq`, `PartialOrd`, `Ord`, `Hash`, and `Display`. It wraps `Arc<Path>` for cheap cloning.

`StatusEntry` staging state is derived from comparing `index_status` and `worktree_status` — provide a helper method `staging_state()` or similar.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All listed types are defined with appropriate derives.
- [ ] `RepoPath` implements `Ord`, `Hash`, `Display`, and `From<&str>` / `From<PathBuf>`.
- [ ] `StatusEntry` has a method to derive staging state (fully staged, fully unstaged, partially staged).
- [ ] `FileStatus` enum covers Added, Modified, Deleted, Untracked, Unchanged.
- [ ] `cargo check` passes.

## Design Considerations

- Use `Arc<Path>` inside `RepoPath` for cheap cloning — these are passed around frequently.
- Keep types `Send + Sync` since they cross thread boundaries via the job queue.
- Don't add SumTree integration in this issue — that's an optimization for later.
- `BlameEntry` uses `String` for SHA rather than a git2-specific OID type to keep the pure layer backend-agnostic.

## Out of Scope

- SumTree `Item` / `Summary` implementations for `StatusEntry` — future optimization.
- Conversion between `RepoPath` and absolute/project paths — handled by GitStore (issue #9).

## Test Guidance

**Primary category:** Unit tests

**What to test:**

- `RepoPath` ordering matches lexicographic path ordering.
- `RepoPath` round-trips through `Display` / `From<&str>`.
- `StatusEntry::staging_state()` returns correct state for all combinations of `index_status` and `worktree_status`.

**Boundary conditions:**

- `RepoPath` with empty path, paths with special characters, nested paths.
- `GitFileStatus` where both `index_status` and `worktree_status` are `Unchanged`.

**Infrastructure needs:**

- None.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Implementation is complete and builds without errors.
- [ ] All new and existing tests pass in CI.
- [ ] Code has been reviewed and approved by at least one other engineer.
- [ ] Changes are documented — at minimum, meaningful commit messages and inline comments where intent is non-obvious.
- [ ] All success criteria listed in the issue are met.

    10→## Summary
    11→
    12→Define the `GitError` enum in `src/git/error.rs` using `thiserror`. This provides structured, descriptive error types for all git operations, wrapping `git2::Error`, `std::io::Error`, and CLI process failures.
    13→
    14→## User Story
    15→
    16→As a developer implementing git operations, I want a unified error type so that callers get structured, debuggable errors regardless of whether the operation used git2 or the CLI.
    17→
    18→## Detailed Description
    19→
    20→```rust
    21→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
    22→pub enum GitError {
    23→    #[error("git2 error: {0}")]
    24→    Git2(#[from] git2::Error),
    25→
    26→    #[error("IO error: {0}")]
    27→    Io(#[from] std::io::Error),
    28→
    29→    #[error("git CLI failed (exit {exit_code}): {stderr}")]
    30→    CliError { exit_code: i32, stderr: String },
    31→
    32→    #[error("repository not found at {path}")]
    33→    RepoNotFound { path: PathBuf },
    34→
    35→    #[error("path not found in repository: {0}")]
    36→    PathNotFound(RepoPath),
    37→
    38→    #[error("invalid operation: {0}")]
    39→    InvalidOperation(String),
    40→
    41→    #[error("merge conflict in {path}")]
    42→    MergeConflict { path: RepoPath },
    43→}
    44→
    45→pub type GitResult<T> = Result<T, GitError>;
    46→```
    47→
    48→## Success Criteria
    49→
    50→- [ ] `GitError` enum is defined with `thiserror` derives.
    51→- [ ] `From<git2::Error>` and `From<std::io::Error>` conversions exist.
    52→- [ ] `GitResult<T>` type alias is defined.
    53→- [ ] Error messages include enough context for debugging (paths, exit codes, stderr).
    54→- [ ] `cargo check` passes.
    55→
    56→## Design Considerations
    57→
    58→- Use `thiserror` which is already in `Cargo.toml`.
    59→- Keep variants focused on categories of failure, not one variant per method.
    60→- `CliError` captures stderr from git subprocess failures — essential for debugging push/pull issues.
    61→- Consider `#[error(transparent)]` for wrapped errors if the inner message is sufficient.
    62→
    63→## Out of Scope
    64→
    65→- Error recovery strategies — callers decide how to handle errors.
    66→- UI error display — that's the panel layer's responsibility.
    67→
    68→## Test Guidance
    69→
    70→**Primary category:** Unit tests
    71→
    72→**What to test:**
    73→
    74→- `GitError::from(git2::Error)` produces a `Git2` variant.
    75→- `GitError::from(io::Error)` produces an `Io` variant.
    76→- Error `Display` output includes relevant context.
    77→
    78→**Boundary conditions:**
    79→
    80→- `CliError` with empty stderr.
    81→- `CliError` with multi-line stderr.
    82→
    83→**Infrastructure needs:**
    84→
    85→- None.
    86→
    87→## Definition of Done
    88→
    89→- [ ] Implementation is complete and builds without errors.
    90→- [ ] All new and existing tests pass in CI.
    91→- [ ] Code has been reviewed and approved by at least one other engineer.
    92→- [ ] Changes are documented — at minimum, meaningful commit messages and inline comments where intent is non-obvious.
    93→- [ ] All success criteria listed in the issue are met.

# Error Types

| Field        | Value                                                              |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Type**     | `feature`                                                          |
| **Priority** | `p1`                                                               |
| **Parent**   | [Git Abstraction Layer](feature-git-abstraction-layer.md)          |
| **Created**  | 2026-03-13                                                         |

## Summary

Define the `GitError` enum in `src/git/error.rs` using `thiserror`. This provides structured, descriptive error types for all git operations, wrapping `git2::Error`, `std::io::Error`, and CLI process failures.

## User Story

As a developer implementing git operations, I want a unified error type so that callers get structured, debuggable errors regardless of whether the operation used git2 or the CLI.

## Detailed Description

```rust
#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
pub enum GitError {
    #[error("git2 error: {0}")]
    Git2(#[from] git2::Error),

    #[error("IO error: {0}")]
    Io(#[from] std::io::Error),

    #[error("git CLI failed (exit {exit_code}): {stderr}")]
    CliError { exit_code: i32, stderr: String },

    #[error("repository not found at {path}")]
    RepoNotFound { path: PathBuf },

    #[error("path not found in repository: {0}")]
    PathNotFound(RepoPath),

    #[error("invalid operation: {0}")]
    InvalidOperation(String),

    #[error("merge conflict in {path}")]
    MergeConflict { path: RepoPath },
}

pub type GitResult<T> = Result<T, GitError>;
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `GitError` enum is defined with `thiserror` derives.
- [ ] `From<git2::Error>` and `From<std::io::Error>` conversions exist.
- [ ] `GitResult<T>` type alias is defined.
- [ ] Error messages include enough context for debugging (paths, exit codes, stderr).
- [ ] `cargo check` passes.

## Design Considerations

- Use `thiserror` which is already in `Cargo.toml`.
- Keep variants focused on categories of failure, not one variant per method.
- `CliError` captures stderr from git subprocess failures — essential for debugging push/pull issues.
- Consider `#[error(transparent)]` for wrapped errors if the inner message is sufficient.

## Out of Scope

- Error recovery strategies — callers decide how to handle errors.
- UI error display — that's the panel layer's responsibility.

## Test Guidance

**Primary category:** Unit tests

**What to test:**

- `GitError::from(git2::Error)` produces a `Git2` variant.
- `GitError::from(io::Error)` produces an `Io` variant.
- Error `Display` output includes relevant context.

**Boundary conditions:**

- `CliError` with empty stderr.
- `CliError` with multi-line stderr.

**Infrastructure needs:**

- None.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Implementation is complete and builds without errors.
- [ ] All new and existing tests pass in CI.
- [ ] Code has been reviewed and approved by at least one other engineer.
- [ ] Changes are documented — at minimum, meaningful commit messages and inline comments where intent is non-obvious.
- [ ] All success criteria listed in the issue are met.

1→# Repository Trait
     2→
     3→| Field        | Value                                                              |
     4→| ------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
     5→| **Type**     | `feature`                                                          |
     6→| **Priority** | `p1`                                                               |
     7→| **Parent**   | [Git Abstraction Layer](feature-git-abstraction-layer.md)          |
     8→| **Created**  | 2026-03-13                                                         |
     9→
    10→## Summary
    11→
    12→Define the `GitRepository` trait in `src/git/repository.rs` with ~40 methods covering identity, staging, commit, remote ops, status, branches, diff/content, blame, stash, history, and conflict resolution. This is the central abstraction that both `RealGitRepository` and `FakeGitRepository` implement.
    13→
    14→## User Story
    15→
    16→As a developer building the git entity layer, I want a single trait that defines all git operations so that I can swap between real and fake implementations for testing.
    17→
    18→## Detailed Description
    19→
    20→The trait should be `Send + Sync` and use only types from `src/git/types.rs` and `src/git/error.rs`. It must have no GPUI dependencies.
    21→
    22→### Method groups (~40 methods):
    23→
    24→**Identity (2):**
    25→- `fn path(&self) -> &Path` — .git directory path
    26→- `fn work_directory(&self) -> Option<&Path>`
    27→
    28→**Staging/Index (4):**
    29→- `fn stage_paths(&self, paths: &[RepoPath], env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<()>`
    30→- `fn unstage_paths(&self, paths: &[RepoPath], env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<()>`
    31→- `fn set_index_text(&self, path: &RepoPath, content: Option<String>, env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<()>`
    32→- `fn reload_index(&self)`
    33→
    34→**Commit (2):**
    35→- `fn commit(&self, message: &str, options: &CommitOptions, env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<()>`
    36→- `fn uncommit(&self, env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<()>`
    37→
    38→**Remote (4):**
    39→- `fn push(&self, branch: &str, remote: Option<&str>, options: &PushOptions, env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<()>`
    40→- `fn pull(&self, rebase: bool, env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<()>`
    41→- `fn fetch(&self, env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<()>`
    42→- `fn create_remote(&self, name: &str, url: &str) -> GitResult<()>`
    43→
    44→**Status (2):**
    45→- `fn status(&self, path_prefixes: &[RepoPath]) -> GitResult<Vec<StatusEntry>>`
    46→- `fn status_for_path(&self, path: &RepoPath) -> GitResult<Option<StatusEntry>>`
    47→
    48→**Branch (6):**
    49→- `fn current_branch(&self) -> Option<Branch>`
    50→- `fn branches(&self) -> GitResult<Vec<Branch>>`
    51→- `fn create_branch(&self, name: &str) -> GitResult<()>`
    52→- `fn checkout(&self, target: &str, env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<()>`
    53→- `fn delete_branch(&self, name: &str) -> GitResult<()>`
    54→- `fn merge_base(&self, a: &str, b: &str) -> GitResult<Option<String>>`
    55→- `fn remote_url(&self, name: &str) -> Option<String>`
    56→
    57→**Diff/Content (2):**
    58→- `fn head_text_for_path(&self, path: &RepoPath) -> GitResult<Option<String>>`
    59→- `fn index_text_for_path(&self, path: &RepoPath) -> GitResult<Option<String>>`
    60→
    61→**Blame (1):**
    62→- `fn blame_for_path(&self, path: &RepoPath, content: &str) -> GitResult<Vec<BlameEntry>>`
    63→
    64→**Stash (5):**
    65→- `fn stash_list(&self) -> GitResult<Vec<StashEntry>>`
    66→- `fn stash_all(&self, message: Option<&str>) -> GitResult<()>`
    67→- `fn stash_pop(&self, index: usize) -> GitResult<()>`
    68→- `fn stash_apply(&self, index: usize) -> GitResult<()>`
    69→- `fn stash_drop(&self, index: usize) -> GitResult<()>`
    70→
    71→**History (2):**
    72→- `fn log(&self, path: Option<&RepoPath>, limit: usize) -> GitResult<Vec<CommitSummary>>`
    73→- `fn show(&self, oid: &str) -> GitResult<CommitDetails>`
    74→
    75→**Conflict (1):**
    76→- `fn checkout_conflict_path(&self, path: &RepoPath, side: ConflictSide) -> GitResult<()>`
    77→
    78→## Success Criteria
    79→
    80→- [ ] `GitRepository` trait is defined with `Send + Sync` bounds.
    81→- [ ] All ~40 methods are declared with correct signatures using project types.
    82→- [ ] No GPUI or async types in the trait — purely synchronous.
    83→- [ ] The `env` parameter is present on all mutating operations.
    84→- [ ] `cargo check` passes.
    85→
    86→## Design Considerations
    87→
    88→- The trait is intentionally synchronous. Async wrapping happens in the Repository entity (issue #8).
    89→- `env` parameter on mutating methods supports credential helper injection via `GIT_ASKPASS`.
    90→- Use `&str` for content parameters passed to blame rather than `Rope` to keep the pure layer free of editor types.
    91→- Consider using `&[RepoPath]` rather than owned `Vec` for input parameters.
    92→
    93→## Out of Scope
    94→
    95→- Implementing any methods — that's issues #5 (read-only), #6 (mutating), and #7 (fake).
    96→- Default method implementations — keep the trait pure.
    97→
    98→## Test Guidance
    99→
   100→**Primary category:** Integration tests
   101→
   102→**What to test:**
   103→
   104→- Trait is object-safe: `Arc<dyn GitRepository>` compiles.
   105→- All method signatures use only types from this crate.
   106→
   107→**Boundary conditions:**
   108→
   109→- Ensure `dyn GitRepository` can be used as `Send + Sync` trait object.
   110→
   111→**Infrastructure needs:**
   112→
   113→- None — this is a trait definition only.
   114→

   116→
   117→- [ ] Implementation is complete and builds without errors.
   118→- [ ] All new and existing tests pass in CI.

# Repository Trait

| Field        | Value                                                              |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Type**     | `feature`                                                          |
| **Priority** | `p1`                                                               |
| **Parent**   | [Git Abstraction Layer](feature-git-abstraction-layer.md)          |
| **Created**  | 2026-03-13                                                         |

## Summary

Define the `GitRepository` trait in `src/git/repository.rs` with ~40 methods covering identity, staging, commit, remote ops, status, branches, diff/content, blame, stash, history, and conflict resolution. This is the central abstraction that both `RealGitRepository` and `FakeGitRepository` implement.

## User Story

As a developer building the git entity layer, I want a single trait that defines all git operations so that I can swap between real and fake implementations for testing.

## Detailed Description

The trait should be `Send + Sync` and use only types from `src/git/types.rs` and `src/git/error.rs`. It must have no GPUI dependencies.

### Method groups (~40 methods):

**Identity (2):**
- `fn path(&self) -> &Path` — .git directory path
- `fn work_directory(&self) -> Option<&Path>`

**Staging/Index (4):**
- `fn stage_paths(&self, paths: &[RepoPath], env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<()>`
- `fn unstage_paths(&self, paths: &[RepoPath], env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<()>`
- `fn set_index_text(&self, path: &RepoPath, content: Option<String>, env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<()>`
- `fn reload_index(&self)`

**Commit (2):**
- `fn commit(&self, message: &str, options: &CommitOptions, env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<()>`
- `fn uncommit(&self, env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<()>`

**Remote (4):**
- `fn push(&self, branch: &str, remote: Option<&str>, options: &PushOptions, env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<()>`
- `fn pull(&self, rebase: bool, env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<()>`
- `fn fetch(&self, env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<()>`
- `fn create_remote(&self, name: &str, url: &str) -> GitResult<()>`

**Status (2):**
- `fn status(&self, path_prefixes: &[RepoPath]) -> GitResult<Vec<StatusEntry>>`
- `fn status_for_path(&self, path: &RepoPath) -> GitResult<Option<StatusEntry>>`

**Branch (6):**
- `fn current_branch(&self) -> Option<Branch>`
- `fn branches(&self) -> GitResult<Vec<Branch>>`
- `fn create_branch(&self, name: &str) -> GitResult<()>`
- `fn checkout(&self, target: &str, env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<()>`
- `fn delete_branch(&self, name: &str) -> GitResult<()>`
- `fn merge_base(&self, a: &str, b: &str) -> GitResult<Option<String>>`
- `fn remote_url(&self, name: &str) -> Option<String>`

**Diff/Content (2):**
- `fn head_text_for_path(&self, path: &RepoPath) -> GitResult<Option<String>>`
- `fn index_text_for_path(&self, path: &RepoPath) -> GitResult<Option<String>>`

**Blame (1):**
- `fn blame_for_path(&self, path: &RepoPath, content: &str) -> GitResult<Vec<BlameEntry>>`

**Stash (5):**
- `fn stash_list(&self) -> GitResult<Vec<StashEntry>>`
- `fn stash_all(&self, message: Option<&str>) -> GitResult<()>`
- `fn stash_pop(&self, index: usize) -> GitResult<()>`
- `fn stash_apply(&self, index: usize) -> GitResult<()>`
- `fn stash_drop(&self, index: usize) -> GitResult<()>`

**History (2):**
- `fn log(&self, path: Option<&RepoPath>, limit: usize) -> GitResult<Vec<CommitSummary>>`
- `fn show(&self, oid: &str) -> GitResult<CommitDetails>`

**Conflict (1):**
- `fn checkout_conflict_path(&self, path: &RepoPath, side: ConflictSide) -> GitResult<()>`

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `GitRepository` trait is defined with `Send + Sync` bounds.
- [ ] All ~40 methods are declared with correct signatures using project types.
- [ ] No GPUI or async types in the trait — purely synchronous.
- [ ] The `env` parameter is present on all mutating operations.
- [ ] `cargo check` passes.

## Design Considerations

- The trait is intentionally synchronous. Async wrapping happens in the Repository entity (issue #8).
- `env` parameter on mutating methods supports credential helper injection via `GIT_ASKPASS`.
- Use `&str` for content parameters passed to blame rather than `Rope` to keep the pure layer free of editor types.
- Consider using `&[RepoPath]` rather than owned `Vec` for input parameters.

## Out of Scope

- Implementing any methods — that's issues #5 (read-only), #6 (mutating), and #7 (fake).
- Default method implementations — keep the trait pure.

## Test Guidance

**Primary category:** Integration tests

**What to test:**

- Trait is object-safe: `Arc<dyn GitRepository>` compiles.
- All method signatures use only types from this crate.

**Boundary conditions:**

- Ensure `dyn GitRepository` can be used as `Send + Sync` trait object.

**Infrastructure needs:**

- None — this is a trait definition only.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Implementation is complete and builds without errors.
- [ ] All new and existing tests pass in CI.
- [ ] Code has been reviewed and approved by at least one other engineer.
- [ ] Changes are documented — at minimum, meaningful commit messages and inline comments where intent is non-obvious.
- [ ] All success criteria listed in the issue are met.

1→# Real Repository — Read-Only Methods
     2→
     3→| Field        | Value                                                              |
     4→| ------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
     5→| **Type**     | `feature`                                                          |
     6→| **Priority** | `p1`                                                               |

     8→| **Created**  | 2026-03-13                                                         |
     9→
    10→## Summary
    11→
    12→Implement `RealGitRepository` struct and its read-only `GitRepository` trait methods using git2: identity, status, branch queries, blame, log/show, stash list, head/index text retrieval, and merge base. Mutating methods are stubbed with `todo!()` for issue #6.
    13→
    14→## User Story
    15→
    16→As a developer, I want to read repository state (status, branches, blame, log) through the trait so that the entity layer can display git information without direct git2 usage.
    17→
    18→## Detailed Description
    19→
    20→### Struct definition
    21→
    22→```rust
    23→pub struct RealGitRepository {
    24→    repository: Mutex<git2::Repository>,
    25→    path: PathBuf,
    26→    work_directory: Option<PathBuf>,
    27→}
    28→```
    29→
    30→`git2::Repository` is not `Sync`, so it must be wrapped in `Mutex`. The `path` and `work_directory` are cached at construction time to avoid locking for identity queries.
    31→
    32→### Constructor
    33→
    34→```rust
    35→impl RealGitRepository {
    36→    pub fn open(path: &Path) -> GitResult<Self> {
    37→        let repo = git2::Repository::open(path)?;
    38→        // cache work_directory and path
    39→    }
    40→}
    41→```
    42→
    43→### Read-only methods to implement
    44→
    45→- **`path()`** / **`work_directory()`** — return cached values, no lock needed.
    46→- **`status()`** — use `repo.statuses()` with `StatusOptions::include_untracked(true)`, map `git2::Status` bitmask to `GitFileStatus`.
    47→- **`status_for_path()`** — filter from status or use `repo.status_file()`.
    48→- **`current_branch()`** — `repo.head()` → `reference.shorthand()`.
    49→- **`branches()`** — `repo.branches(None)` → map to `Branch` structs.
    50→- **`merge_base()`** — `repo.merge_base(oid_a, oid_b)`.
    51→- **`remote_url()`** — `repo.find_remote(name)` → `.url()`.
    52→- **`head_text_for_path()`** — `repo.head()` → tree → blob → content as UTF-8.
    53→- **`index_text_for_path()`** — `repo.index()` → `get_path()` → `repo.find_blob()` → content.
    54→- **`blame_for_path()`** — `repo.blame_file()` → map hunks to `BlameEntry`.
    55→- **`stash_list()`** — `repo.stash_foreach()` → collect into `Vec<StashEntry>`.
    56→- **`log()`** — `repo.revwalk()` → iterate and collect `CommitSummary`.
    57→- **`show()`** — `repo.find_commit()` → `CommitDetails` with diff stats.
    58→- **`reload_index()`** — `repo.index()?.read(true)`.
    59→
    60→### Stubbed methods
    61→
    62→All mutating methods (`stage_paths`, `unstage_paths`, `set_index_text`, `commit`, `uncommit`, `push`, `pull`, `fetch`, `create_remote`, `create_branch`, `checkout`, `delete_branch`, `stash_all/pop/apply/drop`, `checkout_conflict_path`) should be `todo!("implemented in issue #6")`.
    63→
    64→## Success Criteria
    65→
    66→- [ ] `RealGitRepository` struct is defined with `Mutex<git2::Repository>`.
    67→- [ ] All read-only trait methods are implemented using git2.
    68→- [ ] Mutating methods are stubbed with `todo!()`.
    69→- [ ] Status mapping correctly translates git2 bitmask to `GitFileStatus`.
    70→- [ ] `cargo check` passes.
    71→- [ ] Tests pass against a real temporary git repo.
    72→
    73→## Design Considerations
    74→
    75→- Lock the `Mutex` only for the duration of each method call — don't hold it across methods.
    76→- `git2::Repository::statuses()` can be slow on large repos; this is fine since it runs on a background thread via the job queue.
    77→- Use `repo.revwalk()` with `set_sorting(Sort::TIME)` for log.
    78→- Return `Ok(None)` rather than errors for missing paths in `head_text_for_path` / `index_text_for_path`.
    79→- For blame, pass `content` as `&str` not `Rope` — the trait is editor-type-free.
    80→
    81→## Out of Scope
    82→
    83→- Mutating operations — issue #6.
    84→- Performance optimization (caching, incremental status) — future work.
    85→- SumTree integration for status — future optimization.
    86→
    87→## Test Guidance
    88→
    89→**Primary category:** Integration tests
    90→
    91→**What to test:**
    92→
    93→- Open a real temporary git repo (use `tempfile` + `git2::Repository::init`).
    94→- `status()` on a repo with added, modified, deleted, and untracked files.
    95→- `current_branch()` returns `"main"` (or default branch) on a fresh repo.
    96→- `head_text_for_path()` returns committed file content.
    97→- `index_text_for_path()` returns staged file content.
    98→- `log()` returns commit history in reverse chronological order.
    99→- `blame_for_path()` returns correct line attributions.
   100→
   101→**Boundary conditions:**
   102→
   103→- Empty repository (no commits): status, branch, and log should handle gracefully.
   104→- Binary files: `head_text_for_path` should handle or error clearly.
   105→- Large number of status entries.
   106→
   107→**Infrastructure needs:**
   108→
   109→- `tempfile` crate for temporary directories.
   110→- `git2::Repository::init()` for creating test repos.
   111→- Helper functions to create commits, add files, etc. in test repos.
   112→
   113→## Definition of Done
   114→

   116→- [ ] All new and existing tests pass in CI.

# Real Repository — Read-Only Methods

| Field        | Value                                                              |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Type**     | `feature`                                                          |
| **Priority** | `p1`                                                               |
| **Parent**   | [Git Abstraction Layer](feature-git-abstraction-layer.md)          |
| **Created**  | 2026-03-13                                                         |

## Summary

Implement `RealGitRepository` struct and its read-only `GitRepository` trait methods using git2: identity, status, branch queries, blame, log/show, stash list, head/index text retrieval, and merge base. Mutating methods are stubbed with `todo!()` for issue #6.

## User Story

As a developer, I want to read repository state (status, branches, blame, log) through the trait so that the entity layer can display git information without direct git2 usage.

## Detailed Description

### Struct definition

```rust
pub struct RealGitRepository {
    repository: Mutex<git2::Repository>,
    path: PathBuf,
    work_directory: Option<PathBuf>,
}
```

`git2::Repository` is not `Sync`, so it must be wrapped in `Mutex`. The `path` and `work_directory` are cached at construction time to avoid locking for identity queries.

### Constructor

```rust
impl RealGitRepository {
    pub fn open(path: &Path) -> GitResult<Self> {
        let repo = git2::Repository::open(path)?;
        // cache work_directory and path
    }
}
```

### Read-only methods to implement

- **`path()`** / **`work_directory()`** — return cached values, no lock needed.
- **`status()`** — use `repo.statuses()` with `StatusOptions::include_untracked(true)`, map `git2::Status` bitmask to `GitFileStatus`.
- **`status_for_path()`** — filter from status or use `repo.status_file()`.
- **`current_branch()`** — `repo.head()` → `reference.shorthand()`.
- **`branches()`** — `repo.branches(None)` → map to `Branch` structs.
- **`merge_base()`** — `repo.merge_base(oid_a, oid_b)`.
- **`remote_url()`** — `repo.find_remote(name)` → `.url()`.
- **`head_text_for_path()`** — `repo.head()` → tree → blob → content as UTF-8.
- **`index_text_for_path()`** — `repo.index()` → `get_path()` → `repo.find_blob()` → content.
- **`blame_for_path()`** — `repo.blame_file()` → map hunks to `BlameEntry`.
- **`stash_list()`** — `repo.stash_foreach()` → collect into `Vec<StashEntry>`.
- **`log()`** — `repo.revwalk()` → iterate and collect `CommitSummary`.
- **`show()`** — `repo.find_commit()` → `CommitDetails` with diff stats.
- **`reload_index()`** — `repo.index()?.read(true)`.

### Stubbed methods

All mutating methods (`stage_paths`, `unstage_paths`, `set_index_text`, `commit`, `uncommit`, `push`, `pull`, `fetch`, `create_remote`, `create_branch`, `checkout`, `delete_branch`, `stash_all/pop/apply/drop`, `checkout_conflict_path`) should be `todo!("implemented in issue #6")`.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `RealGitRepository` struct is defined with `Mutex<git2::Repository>`.
- [ ] All read-only trait methods are implemented using git2.
- [ ] Mutating methods are stubbed with `todo!()`.
- [ ] Status mapping correctly translates git2 bitmask to `GitFileStatus`.
- [ ] `cargo check` passes.
- [ ] Tests pass against a real temporary git repo.

## Design Considerations

- Lock the `Mutex` only for the duration of each method call — don't hold it across methods.
- `git2::Repository::statuses()` can be slow on large repos; this is fine since it runs on a background thread via the job queue.
- Use `repo.revwalk()` with `set_sorting(Sort::TIME)` for log.
- Return `Ok(None)` rather than errors for missing paths in `head_text_for_path` / `index_text_for_path`.
- For blame, pass `content` as `&str` not `Rope` — the trait is editor-type-free.

## Out of Scope

- Mutating operations — issue #6.
- Performance optimization (caching, incremental status) — future work.
- SumTree integration for status — future optimization.

## Test Guidance

**Primary category:** Integration tests

**What to test:**

- Open a real temporary git repo (use `tempfile` + `git2::Repository::init`).
- `status()` on a repo with added, modified, deleted, and untracked files.
- `current_branch()` returns `"main"` (or default branch) on a fresh repo.
- `head_text_for_path()` returns committed file content.
- `index_text_for_path()` returns staged file content.
- `log()` returns commit history in reverse chronological order.
- `blame_for_path()` returns correct line attributions.

**Boundary conditions:**

- Empty repository (no commits): status, branch, and log should handle gracefully.
- Binary files: `head_text_for_path` should handle or error clearly.
- Large number of status entries.

**Infrastructure needs:**

- `tempfile` crate for temporary directories.
- `git2::Repository::init()` for creating test repos.
- Helper functions to create commits, add files, etc. in test repos.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Implementation is complete and builds without errors.
- [ ] All new and existing tests pass in CI.
- [ ] Code has been reviewed and approved by at least one other engineer.
- [ ] Changes are documented — at minimum, meaningful commit messages and inline comments where intent is non-obvious.
- [ ] All success criteria listed in the issue are met.

content.content:
1→# Real Repository — Mutating Methods
     2→
     3→| Field        | Value                                                              |
     4→| ------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
     5→| **Type**     | `feature`                                                          |
     6→| **Priority** | `p1`                                                               |
     7→| **Parent**   | [Git Abstraction Layer](feature-git-abstraction-layer.md)          |
     8→| **Created**  | 2026-03-13                                                         |
     9→
    10→## Summary
    11→
    12→Implement the mutating `GitRepository` trait methods on `RealGitRepository`: staging/unstaging, index writes, commit/uncommit, push/pull/fetch, branch creation/deletion/checkout, stash operations, remote creation, and conflict resolution. Local index operations use git2; network and complex operations use the git CLI.
    13→
    14→## User Story
    15→
    16→As a developer, I want to perform git write operations through the trait so that staging, committing, pushing, and branch management work through a uniform interface.
    17→
    18→## Detailed Description
    19→
    20→### git2-based methods (local index operations)
    21→
    22→- **`stage_paths()`** — shell out to `git add` via CLI (git2's staging API is limited for directory patterns). Alternatively, use `repo.index()` → `add_path()` → `write()`.
    23→- **`unstage_paths()`** — `git reset HEAD -- <paths>` via CLI, or use git2 to restore index entries from HEAD tree.
    24→- **`set_index_text()`** — `repo.blob(content)` → `index.add()` with constructed `IndexEntry` → `index.write()`. For `None` content: `index.remove_path()` → `index.write()`.
    25→- **`commit()`** — use git2: `repo.index()` → `write_tree()` → `repo.commit()` with HEAD as parent. Handle `CommitOptions::amend` by using `repo.head().target()` as the commit to amend.
    26→- **`uncommit()`** — equivalent to `git reset HEAD^ --soft`: move HEAD to parent commit.
    27→- **`create_branch()`** — `repo.branch(name, &head_commit, false)`.
    28→- **`delete_branch()`** — find branch → `branch.delete()`.
    29→- **`checkout()`** — CLI: `git checkout <target>` (git2's checkout is complex and error-prone).
    30→
    31→### CLI-based methods (network operations)
    32→
    33→These shell out to `git` with the provided `env` map:
    34→
    35→- **`push()`** — `git push [remote] [branch] [--force] [--set-upstream]`
    36→- **`pull()`** — `git pull [--rebase]`
    37→- **`fetch()`** — `git fetch`
    38→- **`create_remote()`** — `git remote add <name> <url>`
    39→
    40→### Stash methods (git2 or CLI)
    41→
    42→- **`stash_all()`** — `git stash push -m <message>` or git2 `repo.stash_save()`.
    43→- **`stash_pop()`** — `git stash pop stash@{index}`.
    44→- **`stash_apply()`** — `git stash apply stash@{index}`.
    45→- **`stash_drop()`** — `git stash drop stash@{index}`.
    46→
    47→### Conflict resolution
    48→
    49→- **`checkout_conflict_path()`** — `git checkout --ours/--theirs <path>`.
    50→
    51→### CLI helper
    52→
    53→Create a private helper for running git commands:
    54→
    55→```rust
    56→fn run_git(&self, args: &[&str], env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<String> {
    57→    let output = Command::new("git")
    58→        .args(args)
    59→        .envs(env)
    60→        .current_dir(self.work_directory.as_ref().unwrap_or(&self.path))
    61→        .output()?;
    62→    if output.status.success() {
    63→        Ok(String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).into_owned())
    64→    } else {
    65→        Err(GitError::CliError {
    66→            exit_code: output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1),
    67→            stderr: String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).into_owned(),
    68→        })
    69→    }
    70→}
    71→```
    72→
    73→## Success Criteria
    74→
    75→- [ ] All mutating trait methods are implemented (replacing `todo!()` stubs from issue #5).
    76→- [ ] Index operations use git2 for speed.
    77→- [ ] Network operations use git CLI for credential helper compatibility.
    78→- [ ] `set_index_text()` correctly writes blob → index entry → index.write().
    79→- [ ] `commit()` handles both normal and amend cases.
    80→- [ ] CLI helper captures stderr into `GitError::CliError`.
    81→- [ ] `cargo check` passes.
    82→
    83→## Design Considerations
    84→
    85→- Prefer git CLI for operations where git2's API is cumbersome or limited (checkout, stash).
    86→- The `env` parameter must be forwarded to all CLI calls — it will contain `GIT_ASKPASS` in the future.
    87→- `set_index_text()` is the mechanism for hunk-level staging: the caller computes desired index content and writes it directly.
    88→- After any index mutation, callers should call `reload_index()`.
    89→
    90→## Out of Scope
    91→
    92→- Credential helper (AskPass) infrastructure — `env` map will be empty for now.
    93→- Remote mode / RPC — local only.
    94→
    95→## Test Guidance
    96→
    97→**Primary category:** Integration tests
    98→
    99→**What to test:**
   100→
   101→- `stage_paths()` moves a file from unstaged to staged (verify with subsequent `status()` call).
   102→- `unstage_paths()` moves a file from staged to unstaged.
   103→- `set_index_text()` writes specific content to the index (verify with `index_text_for_path()`).
   104→- `commit()` creates a new commit (verify with `log()`).
   105→- `commit()` with amend replaces the last commit.
   106→- `uncommit()` moves HEAD back one commit.
   107→- `create_branch()` and `delete_branch()` roundtrip.
   108→- CLI helper returns `CliError` on bad commands.
   109→
   110→**Boundary conditions:**
   111→
   112→- Staging a file that's already staged.
   113→- Committing with an empty index.
   114→- Uncommit on a repo with only one commit (should error).
   115→- `set_index_text(path, None)` removes a path from the index.
   116→
   117→**Infrastructure needs:**
   118→
   119→- Same test repo infrastructure from issue #5.
   120→- Tests for CLI methods need `git` binary available in PATH.
   121→

   123→

   125→- [ ] All new and existing tests pass in CI.
   126→- [ ] Code has been reviewed and approved by at least one other engineer.

# Real Repository — Mutating Methods

| Field        | Value                                                              |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Type**     | `feature`                                                          |
| **Priority** | `p1`                                                               |
| **Parent**   | [Git Abstraction Layer](feature-git-abstraction-layer.md)          |
| **Created**  | 2026-03-13                                                         |

## Summary

Implement the mutating `GitRepository` trait methods on `RealGitRepository`: staging/unstaging, index writes, commit/uncommit, push/pull/fetch, branch creation/deletion/checkout, stash operations, remote creation, and conflict resolution. Local index operations use git2; network and complex operations use the git CLI.

## User Story

As a developer, I want to perform git write operations through the trait so that staging, committing, pushing, and branch management work through a uniform interface.

## Detailed Description

### git2-based methods (local index operations)

- **`stage_paths()`** — shell out to `git add` via CLI (git2's staging API is limited for directory patterns). Alternatively, use `repo.index()` → `add_path()` → `write()`.
- **`unstage_paths()`** — `git reset HEAD -- <paths>` via CLI, or use git2 to restore index entries from HEAD tree.
- **`set_index_text()`** — `repo.blob(content)` → `index.add()` with constructed `IndexEntry` → `index.write()`. For `None` content: `index.remove_path()` → `index.write()`.
- **`commit()`** — use git2: `repo.index()` → `write_tree()` → `repo.commit()` with HEAD as parent. Handle `CommitOptions::amend` by using `repo.head().target()` as the commit to amend.
- **`uncommit()`** — equivalent to `git reset HEAD^ --soft`: move HEAD to parent commit.
- **`create_branch()`** — `repo.branch(name, &head_commit, false)`.
- **`delete_branch()`** — find branch → `branch.delete()`.
- **`checkout()`** — CLI: `git checkout <target>` (git2's checkout is complex and error-prone).

### CLI-based methods (network operations)

These shell out to `git` with the provided `env` map:

- **`push()`** — `git push [remote] [branch] [--force] [--set-upstream]`
- **`pull()`** — `git pull [--rebase]`
- **`fetch()`** — `git fetch`
- **`create_remote()`** — `git remote add <name> <url>`

### Stash methods (git2 or CLI)

- **`stash_all()`** — `git stash push -m <message>` or git2 `repo.stash_save()`.
- **`stash_pop()`** — `git stash pop stash@{index}`.
- **`stash_apply()`** — `git stash apply stash@{index}`.
- **`stash_drop()`** — `git stash drop stash@{index}`.

### Conflict resolution

- **`checkout_conflict_path()`** — `git checkout --ours/--theirs <path>`.

### CLI helper

Create a private helper for running git commands:

```rust
fn run_git(&self, args: &[&str], env: &HashMap<String, String>) -> GitResult<String> {
    let output = Command::new("git")
        .args(args)
        .envs(env)
        .current_dir(self.work_directory.as_ref().unwrap_or(&self.path))
        .output()?;
    if output.status.success() {
        Ok(String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).into_owned())
    } else {
        Err(GitError::CliError {
            exit_code: output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1),
            stderr: String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).into_owned(),
        })
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All mutating trait methods are implemented (replacing `todo!()` stubs from issue #5).
- [ ] Index operations use git2 for speed.
- [ ] Network operations use git CLI for credential helper compatibility.
- [ ] `set_index_text()` correctly writes blob → index entry → index.write().
- [ ] `commit()` handles both normal and amend cases.
- [ ] CLI helper captures stderr into `GitError::CliError`.
- [ ] `cargo check` passes.

## Design Considerations

- Prefer git CLI for operations where git2's API is cumbersome or limited (checkout, stash).
- The `env` parameter must be forwarded to all CLI calls — it will contain `GIT_ASKPASS` in the future.
- `set_index_text()` is the mechanism for hunk-level staging: the caller computes desired index content and writes it directly.
- After any index mutation, callers should call `reload_index()`.

## Out of Scope

- Credential helper (AskPass) infrastructure — `env` map will be empty for now.
- Remote mode / RPC — local only.

## Test Guidance

**Primary category:** Integration tests

**What to test:**

- `stage_paths()` moves a file from unstaged to staged (verify with subsequent `status()` call).
- `unstage_paths()` moves a file from staged to unstaged.
- `set_index_text()` writes specific content to the index (verify with `index_text_for_path()`).
- `commit()` creates a new commit (verify with `log()`).
- `commit()` with amend replaces the last commit.
- `uncommit()` moves HEAD back one commit.
- `create_branch()` and `delete_branch()` roundtrip.
- CLI helper returns `CliError` on bad commands.

**Boundary conditions:**

- Staging a file that's already staged.
- Committing with an empty index.
- Uncommit on a repo with only one commit (should error).
- `set_index_text(path, None)` removes a path from the index.

**Infrastructure needs:**

- Same test repo infrastructure from issue #5.
- Tests for CLI methods need `git` binary available in PATH.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Implementation is complete and builds without errors.
- [ ] All new and existing tests pass in CI.
- [ ] Code has been reviewed and approved by at least one other engineer.
- [ ] Changes are documented — at minimum, meaningful commit messages and inline comments where intent is non-obvious.
- [ ] All success criteria listed in the issue are met.

# Fake Repository Test Double

| Field        | Value                                                              |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Type**     | `feature`                                                          |
| **Priority** | `p2`                                                               |
| **Parent**   | [Git Abstraction Layer](feature-git-abstraction-layer.md)          |
| **Created**  | 2026-03-13                                                         |

## Summary

Implement `FakeGitRepository`, a fully in-memory `GitRepository` implementation for testing. It stores head, index, and working tree state in `HashMap`s and derives status by comparing them — no real git repo or filesystem needed.

## User Story

As a developer writing tests for the entity layer, I want an in-memory git implementation so that tests run fast, are deterministic, and don't require filesystem setup.

## Detailed Description

```rust
pub struct FakeGitRepository {
    /// HEAD state: path → file content
    head: Mutex<HashMap<RepoPath, String>>,
    /// Index state: path → file content
    index: Mutex<HashMap<RepoPath, String>>,
    /// Working tree state: path → file content
    worktree: Mutex<HashMap<RepoPath, String>>,
    /// Current branch name
    current_branch: Mutex<Option<String>>,
    /// All branches
    branches: Mutex<Vec<Branch>>,
    /// Commit log
    commits: Mutex<Vec<CommitSummary>>,
    /// Identity
    path: PathBuf,
    work_directory: Option<PathBuf>,
}
```

### Status derivation

Status is computed by comparing the three maps:

- File in worktree but not in head/index → `Untracked` / `Added`
- File in worktree differs from index → `worktree_status: Modified`
- File in index differs from head → `index_status: Modified` (staged)
- File in head but not in worktree → `Deleted`
- File in index but not in worktree → `worktree_status: Deleted`

### Builder/setter API for test setup

```rust
impl FakeGitRepository {
    pub fn new(path: PathBuf) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn set_head_content(&self, path: RepoPath, content: &str) { ... }
    pub fn set_index_content(&self, path: RepoPath, content: &str) { ... }
    pub fn set_worktree_content(&self, path: RepoPath, content: &str) { ... }
    pub fn set_branch(&self, name: &str) { ... }
    pub fn add_commit(&self, summary: CommitSummary) { ... }
}
```

### Trait implementation behavior

- **`stage_paths()`** — copy content from worktree to index map.
- **`unstage_paths()`** — copy content from head to index map (or remove if not in head).
- **`set_index_text()`** — directly set/remove index map entries.
- **`commit()`** — copy index to head, record a `CommitSummary`.
- **`head_text_for_path()`** — look up in head map.
- **`index_text_for_path()`** — look up in index map.
- **Network operations** (push/pull/fetch) — no-op or return `Ok(())`.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `FakeGitRepository` implements all `GitRepository` trait methods.
- [ ] Status is correctly derived by comparing head, index, and worktree maps.
- [ ] Staging moves content from worktree to index.
- [ ] Committing moves content from index to head.
- [ ] Builder methods allow easy test setup.
- [ ] `cargo test` passes with fake repo tests.

## Design Considerations

- Use `Mutex` on all internal state to satisfy `Send + Sync` requirements.
- Network operations should be no-ops — the fake is for testing local workflows.
- The fake should be realistic enough that `GitStore` and `Repository` entity tests work without modification.
- Consider making `FakeGitRepository` `#[cfg(test)]` or gating behind a feature flag, though for now placing it in the main crate is fine since it has no heavy dependencies.

## Out of Scope

- Fake filesystem integration — use simple HashMaps, not a FakeFs.
- Realistic merge conflict simulation.
- Blame simulation (can return empty vec).

## Test Guidance

**Primary category:** Unit tests

**What to test:**

- Create a fake repo, set head and worktree content, verify `status()` returns correct entries.
- `stage_paths()` followed by `status()` shows files as staged.
- `commit()` followed by `status()` shows clean state.
- `set_index_text()` with specific content is reflected in `index_text_for_path()`.
- `head_text_for_path()` returns content set via `set_head_content()`.

**Boundary conditions:**

- Empty repository (all maps empty).
- File only in worktree (untracked).
- File only in head (deleted in worktree).
- Same content in all three maps (unchanged).

**Infrastructure needs:**

- None — the whole point is no infrastructure.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Implementation is complete and builds without errors.
- [ ] All new and existing tests pass in CI.
- [ ] Code has been reviewed and approved by at least one other engineer.
- [ ] Changes are documented — at minimum, meaningful commit messages and inline comments where intent is non-obvious.
- [ ] All success criteria listed in the issue are met.

1→# Repository Entity
     2→
     3→| Field        | Value                                                              |
     4→| ------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
     5→| **Type**     | `feature`                                                          |
     6→| **Priority** | `p1`                                                               |
     7→| **Parent**   | [Git Abstraction Layer](feature-git-abstraction-layer.md)          |
     8→| **Created**  | 2026-03-13                                                         |
     9→
    10→## Summary
    11→
    12→Implement the `Repository` GPUI entity that wraps `Arc<dyn GitRepository>` with an async job queue. All git operations are serialized through a background worker task, keeping the main thread free. The entity holds status state, a snapshot for cheap reads, and pending ops for optimistic UI.
    13→
    14→## User Story
    15→
    16→As a developer building the git panel, I want a GPUI entity that runs git operations asynchronously and notifies the UI when state changes, so that the panel stays responsive during slow git operations.
    17→
    18→## Detailed Description
    19→
    20→### Struct
    21→
    22→```rust
    23→pub struct Repository {
    24→    // Identity
    25→    pub dot_git_path: PathBuf,
    26→
    27→    // Status
    28→    pub statuses: Vec<StatusEntry>,
    29→    pub snapshot: RepositorySnapshot,
    30→
    31→    // Backend
    32→    git_repo: Arc<dyn GitRepository>,
    33→
    34→    // Job queue
    35→    job_sender: mpsc::UnboundedSender<GitJob>,
    36→    _worker: Task<()>,
    37→
    38→    // Optimistic UI
    39→    pending_ops: HashMap<RepoPath, PendingOp>,
    40→}
    41→```
    42→
    43→### `RepositorySnapshot`
    44→
    45→```rust
    46→#[derive(Clone, Default)]
    47→pub struct RepositorySnapshot {
    48→    pub branch: Option<Branch>,
    49→    pub stash_count: usize,
    50→}
    51→```
    52→
    53→Cheap-to-clone value type for UI reads without locking the entity.
    54→
    55→### `GitJob` enum
    56→
    57→```rust
    58→enum GitJob {
    59→    RefreshStatus { path_prefixes: Vec<RepoPath> },
    60→    Stage { paths: Vec<RepoPath> },
    61→    Unstage { paths: Vec<RepoPath> },
    62→    SetIndexText { path: RepoPath, content: Option<String> },
    63→    Commit { message: String, options: CommitOptions },
    64→    Push { branch: String, remote: Option<String>, options: PushOptions },
    65→    Pull { rebase: bool },
    66→    Fetch,
    67→}
    68→```
    69→
    70→### Background worker
    71→
    72→The worker is spawned in `Repository::new()` and processes jobs sequentially:
    73→
    74→1. Receive job from channel.
    75→2. Execute the corresponding `GitRepository` method on the background thread.
    76→3. After any mutating operation, automatically refresh status.
    77→4. Call `entity.update(cx, |repo, cx| { repo.statuses = ...; cx.notify(); })` to push changes back to the main thread.
    78→
    79→### Public API
    80→
    81→```rust
    82→impl Repository {
    83→    pub fn new(git_repo: Arc<dyn GitRepository>, cx: &mut Context<Self>) -> Self;
    84→    pub fn stage(&mut self, paths: Vec<RepoPath>, cx: &mut Context<Self>);
    85→    pub fn unstage(&mut self, paths: Vec<RepoPath>, cx: &mut Context<Self>);
    86→    pub fn commit(&mut self, message: String, options: CommitOptions, cx: &mut Context<Self>);
    87→    pub fn refresh_status(&self);
    88→    pub fn snapshot(&self) -> &RepositorySnapshot;
    89→    pub fn statuses(&self) -> &[StatusEntry];
    90→    pub fn effective_status(&self, path: &RepoPath) -> Option<StagingState>;
    91→}
    92→```
    93→
    94→`effective_status()` checks `pending_ops` first, falling back to `statuses`.
    95→
    96→## Success Criteria
    97→
    98→- [ ] `Repository` is a GPUI entity (`impl EventEmitter<RepositoryEvent> for Repository`).
    99→- [ ] Background worker serializes all git operations.
   100→- [ ] Status is automatically refreshed after mutating operations.
   101→- [ ] `cx.notify()` is called when status changes, triggering UI re-renders.
   102→- [ ] `RepositorySnapshot` provides cheap cloneable state for renders.
   103→- [ ] `cargo check` passes.
   104→
   105→## Design Considerations
   106→
   107→- Use `mpsc::unbounded_channel` for the job queue — bounded channels risk deadlock if the worker is busy.
   108→- The worker task must be stored in the struct (`_worker: Task<()>`) to keep it alive.
   109→- Use `WeakEntity` in the worker closure to avoid preventing entity cleanup.
   110→- `cx.notify()` is the mechanism that triggers GPUI re-renders when state changes.
   111→- Consider emitting `RepositoryEvent::IndexChanged` after staging operations so GitStore can refresh buffer diffs.
   112→
   113→## Out of Scope
   114→
   115→- GitStore integration — issue #9.
   116→- Optimistic UI (`pending_ops`) — issue #10.
   117→- Buffer diff management — issues #11–#12.
   118→
   119→## Test Guidance
   120→
   121→**Primary category:** Integration tests
   122→
   123→**What to test:**
   124→
   125→- Create a `Repository` with `FakeGitRepository`, call `stage()`, verify status updates after job processes.
   126→- `refresh_status()` correctly populates `statuses` from the backend.
   127→- `commit()` creates a commit and refreshes status.
   128→- Multiple jobs are processed in order.
   129→
   130→**Boundary conditions:**
   131→
   132→- Entity dropped while worker has pending jobs — should not panic.
   133→- Rapid successive `stage()` / `unstage()` calls.
   134→
   135→**Infrastructure needs:**
   136→
   137→- GPUI test harness (`gpui::test` feature).
   138→- `FakeGitRepository` from issue #7.
   139→
   140→## Definition of Done
   141→
   142→- [ ] Implementation is complete and builds without errors.
   143→- [ ] All new and existing tests pass in CI.
   144→- [ ] Code has been reviewed and approved by at least one other engineer.
   145→- [ ] Changes are documented — at minimum, meaningful commit messages and inline comments where intent is non-obvious.
   146→- [ ] All success criteria listed in the issue are met.
   147→
